principles: Clarify boundaries, data flow and failure modes before implementation.
Readable code and explicit contracts keep teams moving safely.
Authentication, authorization and validation are design inputs.
Engineering decisions should connect to a useful product outcome.
Measure, reflect and turn every build into a better mental model.
skills: OOP, collections, streams and concurrency fundamentals.
Modular REST services with production-minded conventions.
JWT authentication, RBAC and secure request pipelines.
Relational modeling, transactions and query optimization.
Explicit entity mapping and efficient persistence patterns.
Cache-aside strategies and short-lived session data.
Focused unit tests and isolated service behavior.
Resource modeling, versioning and predictable errors.
Automated checks and repeatable delivery pipelines.
Cloud deployment fundamentals and managed services.

projects.name: Furama Resort Platform
projects.subtitle: A secure reservation and operations API for a multi-service hospitality business.
projects.problem: Reservation workflows often fragment customer, contract and facility data across manual processes. Furama centralizes the lifecycle while preserving clear ownership and consistent business rules.
projects.goal: Provide staff with one reliable operational source of truth and expose secure APIs that can support multiple client applications.
projects.database: The relational model separates customers, facilities, contracts and attached services. Foreign keys protect ownership, composite indexes support date and status queries, and transactional boundaries keep bookings consistent.
security: Short-lived JWT access tokens and rotating refresh tokens
Role-based access for ADMIN, MANAGER and STAFF
BCrypt password hashing and bean validation
performance: Database indexes on booking date and status
DTO projections to avoid unnecessary entity loading
projects.lesson: The hardest part was not adding endpoints—it was deciding where reservation rules belonged so the same constraints held across every entry point.

projects.name: Commerce Order Service
projects.subtitle: A modular order workflow designed for consistency, failure recovery and future event-driven growth.
projects.problem: Order placement touches inventory, pricing and payment. A naïve request chain creates partial updates and makes failures difficult to understand.
projects.goal: Protect order consistency with explicit state transitions while keeping modules replaceable as traffic and business complexity grow.
projects.database: Orders and order items use immutable price snapshots. Optimistic locking guards concurrent updates, while idempotency keys prevent duplicate checkouts during client retries.
security: Validated state transitions reject invalid operations
performance: Purpose-built indexes for customer order history
projects.lesson: Modeling order states explicitly turned scattered conditionals into a workflow the team could reason about, test and safely extend.

projects.name: TaskFlow Collaboration API
projects.subtitle: A team workspace backend with granular access control and an auditable activity model.
projects.problem: Collaborative tools need more than CRUD. Membership, permissions and historical context must remain correct as teams and projects evolve.
projects.goal: Create a maintainable collaboration core where authorization is predictable and every meaningful change can be traced.
projects.database: Workspace membership is modeled independently from users and projects. Audit events are append-only, and unique constraints prevent duplicate membership records.
security: OWNER, ADMIN, MEMBER and VIEWER roles
Resource ownership validated at service boundaries
Append-only audit events for sensitive operations
performance: Query plans checked for common dashboards
projects.lesson: Authorization is easiest to trust when expressed as a small set of reusable policies, rather than being scattered across controllers.

timeline: Adapted to a new environment and developed the discipline, patience and communication habits that now shape my engineering work.
Started with algorithms and object-oriented programming, learning to break ambiguous problems into small, testable parts.
Went deeper into the Java ecosystem and learned to model business information with normalized relational databases.
Moved from isolated programs to layered web services with REST contracts, persistence and authentication.
Added caching, containers, testing and security practices while turning projects into complete engineering case studies.
Studying architecture and operational trade-offs while seeking a backend team where careful engineering creates real value.
knowledge: Furama is a reservation and operations platform built with Java, Spring Boot, MySQL and Redis. Its main engineering focus is keeping contracts and facility availability consistent through clear service boundaries and transactions.

Linh treats security as an architecture concern: short-lived JWT access tokens, rotating refresh tokens, BCrypt password hashes, role-based policies, request validation and consistent error responses.
The projects use normalized MySQL models, explicit foreign keys and transaction boundaries. Indexes follow real access patterns such as booking dates, status and workspace membership.
The core stack is Java, Spring Boot, Spring Security, MySQL, JPA and REST. Linh also applies Redis, Docker, JUnit/Mockito and is developing deeper AWS and CI/CD experience.
Performance work starts with access patterns: pagination, purpose-built indexes, DTO projections, query-plan review and Redis cache-aside where staleness can be controlled.
Phan Tuan Linh is a backend-focused engineer building production-minded Java and Spring systems. His approach prioritizes clear architecture, maintainable code, secure defaults and business value.
The portfolio contains 7 case studies: Furama, Commerce Order Service, TaskFlow, Smart Parking Management, Primary School Management, Fashion Shoe Shop Manager and Furama Training Modules 3-5.
Smart Parking Management handles slot status, tickets, license plates, checkout, payment and reports. Its backend focus is clear state transitions, Redis availability and operational audit trails.
Primary School Management covers teachers, students, classes, timetables, message boards and PDF export. It shows team delivery, JWT authorization and modular school workflows.
Fashion Shoe Shop Manager covers product search, cart, checkout, JWT login, Firebase images and PayPal payment history as an end-to-end commerce case.
The old PDF CV is replaced by the Engineering Resume inside resume/. It keeps the same portfolio design system and focuses on backend thinking, projects, stack and learning journey.
Linh is job ready through Java/Spring fundamentals, practical management systems, clear database thinking, secure defaults, fast learning and discipline shaped by Japan experience.
You can contact Linh at skyfreedom45@gmail.com. The portfolio positions him for Java Backend Developer work with REST APIs, Spring Boot, MySQL, JWT and systems thinking.
